content: User prefers pnpm over npm for package management
Always use integration tests with real databases, never mock the data layer
Auth system uses Passport.js with JWT tokens
Deploy to staging first via GitHub Actions, production requires manual approval
Use vitest as the test runner, not jest
Project uses PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM
Error responses should use RFC 7807 Problem Details format
All API endpoints require authentication except /health and /docs
Use zod for runtime schema validation at API boundaries
Redis is used for session storage and rate limiting only
Feature branches should be named feat/TICKET-description
Never commit .env files, use .env.example as template
The frontend uses React with TanStack Router
CSS uses Tailwind with the custom design tokens
All database migrations go through Drizzle Kit
Logging uses structured JSON via pino
User prefers terse responses without trailing summaries
WebSocket connections use socket.io with Redis adapter for scaling
File uploads go to S3 with presigned URLs, never through the API server
Cron jobs are managed by BullMQ with Redis backend
Use ESM imports with .js extensions in all TypeScript source files
Prefer named exports over default exports in all modules
Use const assertions (as const) for literal type narrowing
Arrow functions are preferred over function declarations for callbacks
Use destructuring assignment for object and array unpacking
Use nullish coalescing (??) instead of logical OR (||) for defaults
Use template literals instead of string concatenation
Use Map and Set for collections that need key uniqueness or ordered iteration
Use Record<string, T> for typed object maps instead of plain objects
Use import type syntax for type-only imports to avoid runtime overhead
Use crypto.randomUUID() for generating unique identifiers
Use Array.from() to convert iterables to arrays instead of spread
Use structuredClone() for deep copying objects instead of JSON roundtrip
The no-any rule is enforced; use unknown and narrow with type guards
Use localeCompare for string sorting to handle unicode correctly
Use Temporal API or dayjs for all date arithmetic, never raw Date math
Use named capture groups in regular expressions for readability
Custom errors extend AppError class which wraps code and message
Use Promise.allSettled when failures of individual promises should not abort the batch
Implement exponential backoff with jitter for all retry logic
Embedding model is all-MiniLM-L6-v2 producing 384-dimensional vectors
Hot tier maximum capacity is 50 entries before overflow to warm
Warm tier promotion threshold score is 0.5
Decay half-life for memory scoring is 7 days
Config file format is TOML
SQLite is opened in WAL mode for concurrent read performance
Vector storage uses the sqlite-vec extension
Compaction runs every 24 hours at session end
Cold tier has no entry limit
Embedding dimensions must exactly match 384 or insertion fails
ONNX inference runs locally without any network calls
Default top_k for search is 10 results
Config overrides use deep-merge semantics so nested keys compose
Data directory defaults to ~/.total-recall
Decay-based promote threshold is 0.3 for moving entries to hotter tiers
SQLite BUSY errors appear when multiple processes open the same database file
ONNX model cold start takes 2-3 seconds on first inference call
sqlite-vec extension must be loaded before any vec_ functions are called
better-sqlite3 is synchronous; never use it inside async event handlers expecting non-blocking
MCP stdio transport buffers output; flush with newline-terminated JSON only
sqlite-vec returns distance not similarity; subtract from 1 to get similarity score
Empty embedding vectors cause NaN distances in sqlite-vec queries
TOML arrays require each item on a separate line under the array key
crypto.randomUUID() is only available in Node 19+ or with web crypto polyfill
vitest vi.mock calls must be at the top level of the test file, not inside describe blocks
tsup bundles lose __dirname; use import.meta.url with fileURLToPath instead
sqlite-vec stores vectors as float[384] blobs and requires exact dimension match
MCP error responses must use isError: true flag in the content block
vitest environment variables set in one test file can leak if the env is not re-imported
JSON.stringify key ordering is not guaranteed; use sorted keys for deterministic output
Memory system uses three tiers: hot for recent, warm for active, cold for archived
System exposes memory operations via the MCP protocol
Embedding vectors are stored inside sqlite-vec extension tables in SQLite
Compaction runs at session end to demote stale hot entries to warm
Corpus ingestion is chunked to avoid blocking the event loop on large files
Host memory importer implements a standard interface for pluggable import sources
Retrieval operations log the config snapshot ID for A/B comparison
Benchmark runner seeds corpus into warm tier and cleans up after each run
Config snapshots are stored to enable A/B comparison across runs
Decay scoring drives automatic promotion from cold to warm tier
All tiers share a single SQLite database file
Project detection scopes memories to the nearest package.json directory
Search fans out across multiple tiers in parallel and merges results
Long content is split at sentence boundaries before ingestion
No background daemon process; all operations are synchronous within the MCP call
Tool response content uses JSON encoded inside text content blocks
MCP tool names use snake_case convention
Error responses include an isError flag set to true
Search results include the tier field indicating where the entry was found
memory_store tool returns the generated entry ID
No batch operations are exposed; tools handle one entry at a time
Absent values are represented as undefined not null in tool responses
Input validators are defined in dedicated validator modules
MCP tool description strings must be under 100 characters
Config keys use dot notation for nested access (e.g. tiers.hot.max)
All TypeScript imports use .js file extensions for ESM compatibility
tsup produces a single bundle entry point
No default exports; all exports are named
Test files are colocated next to the source files they test
Type-only imports always use import type syntax
